Table of Contents

Interface IRowBuffer

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geodatabase
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geodatabase.dll

Provides access to members used for getting and modifying a rows values and for getting the fields in the row.

[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
[Guid("93684651-1A83-11D1-8802-0000F877762D")]
public interface IRowBuffer

Properties

Fields

The fields Collection for this row buffer.

IFields Fields { get; }

Property Value

IFields

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>Each table in a database has an ordered collection of fields, there is always at least one field in a table. The ordered collection behaves like a list, so it is possible to access individual fields by a numbered position (or index) in the list.</P> <P>A <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection can also exist independently of a table, for example when creating an index on a table you will need to define which fields take part in the index. There are many interfaces which either require or return a <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection; these include <STRONG>IClass </STRONG>(therefore also <STRONG>IObjectClass </STRONG>and <STRONG>IFeatureClass</STRONG>), <STRONG>ICursor</STRONG>, <STRONG>IIndex</STRONG>, <STRONG>IFeatureClassDraw</STRONG>, <STRONG>IRowBuffer</STRONG>, <STRONG>ISimpleDataConverter</STRONG>, <STRONG>ITableSort </STRONG>and <STRONG>IValidate</STRONG>.</P>

Value[int]

The value of the field with the specified index.

object Value[int Index] { get; set; }

Parameters

Index int

Property Value

object
